commands.review_summary:
The PR records qualifying message-tool-only sends as observed source delivery and propagates that signal through shared auto-reply dispatch, channel visibility helpers, and WhatsApp success handling.

PR surface: Source +48, Tests +76. Total +124 across 15 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: current `main` can mark a message-tool-only run fallback-eligible when no dispatcher final was queued, even though the runner/message tool path can have delivered the visible source reply. I did not run tests because this was a read-only review, but the failure path and added regression tests are clear.
commands.review_followup: - No ClawSweeper repair lane is needed; this is an open maintainer PR with no actionable automated fix identified.

target.title: fix(auto-reply): count message tool sends as delivery
target.body:
## Problem

`message_tool_only` auto-reply runs intentionally suppress the normal dispatcher-owned final reply: the visible response is delivered by the model calling the `message` tool.

That left shared auto-reply with no dispatcher-queued final reply to count. Even when the message tool had already committed visible delivery, `dispatchReplyFromConfig` could return `noVisibleReplyFallbackEligible`, so callers could treat a successful visible reply as a no-reply turn and enter fallback handling.

## Solution

This makes visible delivery an explicit shared auto-reply signal instead of inferring it from channel-specific behavior or raw message-tool telemetry.

- The embedded runner records `didDeliverSourceReplyViaMessageTool` when the existing `message_tool_only` terminal-send predicate accepts a committed `message.send`.
- `runReplyAgent` converts that runner-owned delivery fact into the new generic `onObservedReplyDelivery` callback.
- `dispatchReplyFromConfig` tracks `observedReplyDelivery`, returns it in `DispatchFromConfigResult`, and does not mark the run fallback-eligible when delivery was observed.
- Shared dispatch visibility helpers count `observedReplyDelivery` as visible delivery.
- WhatsApp consumes the shared dispatch result so it treats observed message-tool delivery as success without adding WhatsApp-specific detection logic.

The important boundary is that the delivery decision stays with the runner predicate that already validates the message-tool send: implicit source route, committed delivery evidence, not dry-run, not failed, and not a cross-target send.

## Verification

- Blacksmith Testbox through Crabbox: provider `blacksmith-testbox`, id `tbx_01kt86x1s4rdwkwwp5ph4q2ah3`, Actions run `26926097102`
- `CI=true corepack pnpm test src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/message-tool-terminal.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts src/plugin-sdk/inbound-reply-dispatch.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/auto-reply/monitor/inbound-dispatch.test.ts`
- `CI=true corepack pnpm tsgo:core`
- `CI=true corepack pnpm tsgo:extensions`
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`
